focus: [D4-A3/D4] the exemption is self-judged + post-hoc — define it on the tool, not the intent

graphEvents.label: How can pure transport be exempt without letting design-feeding Open Design reads self-attest after the fact?
The current pure-transport exemption is driven by caller/model intent such as feeds_design_decision, not by inspectable Open Design tool-call data
tool_surface.md says read-only Open Design tools are always safe to surface, while the mandatory design gate says read-only design-feeding calls must block without sk-design
sk-design proof cards prove context shape and READY fields, but not Open Design read-output lineage or whether a pure-transport read was later used as design grounding
Every ambiguous Open Design read must carry an inspectable openDesignUse or receipt before it can be used as design input
A pure-transport receipt may not be cited by a later design-affecting Open Design run or artifact decision

label: The pure-transport exemption is currently caller-asserted intent, not tool-bound policy
recommendation: Add openDesignUse and designInfluence policy to the guarded Open Design tool/proxy surface so ambiguous reads are transport-only unless explicitly marked and gated for design use.
enforcement: ENFORCEABLE for proxy/hook fixtures; ADVISORY for unwrapped upstream tools or transcript-only intent.

label: Read-only Open Design tools are documented as always safe even though some read-only calls are design-feeding and must gate sk-design
recommendation: Split tool policy into mutationSafety and designInfluence axes, and add replay fixtures for list_projects as pure inventory versus design grounding.
enforcement: ENFORCEABLE for docs/schema lint and replay fixtures; ADVISORY until live tool calls expose purpose metadata.

label: sk-design proof cards do not bind Open Design read outputs or pure-transport receipts to later design use
recommendation: Extend skDesignGate and proof cards with OPEN DESIGN RECEIPTS carrying toolName, openDesignUse, outputDigest, usedActiveContext, explicit project, and expiry.
enforcement: ENFORCEABLE when tool outputs/receipts are captured; ADVISORY when proof is only model-authored.

label: Ambiguous Open Design reads require inspectable openDesignUse or read receipts before design use
Pure-transport receipts cannot be reused as design-grounding inputs for later Open Design design-affecting calls

direction: Treat pure transport as a prose exception evaluated after the call
reason: The same read-only tool can be either bare inventory or design grounding, so post-hoc intent does not give a deterministic hook/proxy a stable precondition to enforce.
